reasoning: Applied the error taxonomy (grammar, vocabulary, pragmatic) with severity calibrated against CEFR B2 descriptors. At B2, articles/basic prepositions should be acquired; errors in these areas are moderate-to-major. False friends and null subject omission are expected L1 transfer patterns for Spanish-English (Swan & Smith 2001, Odlin 1989) but at B2 they should be decreasing. Used interlanguage theory to identify fossilized L1 transfer patterns - particularly null subjects (pro-drop), prepositional calques from Spanish "de", and false cognates. Severity is raised for errors that are recurring and expected to be acquired at B2.

errors.original: We had to make a meeting with the regional directors
errors.corrected: We had to hold a meeting with the regional directors
errors.explanation: Collocation error rather than pure tense error - "make a meeting" is a calque from Spanish "hacer una reunion". The verb tense itself is correct.
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: - Spanish "hacer una reunion" directly translated as "make a meeting"; English uses "hold/have a meeting"

errors.original: I had to assist to many meetings
errors.corrected: I had to attend many meetings
errors.explanation: Classic Spanish-English false friend: "asistir" = attend, not assist.
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: - "asistir a una reunion" (Spanish) = attend a meeting; "assist" (English) = help/aid

errors.original: It went quite well actually
errors.corrected: It went quite well, in fact
errors.explanation: Student initially used "actually" in the Spanish sense of "actualmente" (currently), then self-corrected to "in fact".
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: - "actualmente" (Spanish) = currently; "actually" (English) = in fact/really

errors.original: many people think is not enough flexibility
errors.corrected: many people think it is not enough flexibility
errors.explanation: Missing expletive subject "it" in embedded clause.
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: - Spanish is a pro-drop language; "piensan que no es suficiente" omits the subject naturally

errors.original: I am agree with the hybrid model
errors.corrected: I agree with the hybrid model
errors.explanation: Insertion of copula "am" from Spanish structure "estoy de acuerdo".
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: - "estoy de acuerdo" literally = "I am in agreement"; student adds "am" before "agree"

errors.original: It depends of the day
errors.corrected: It depends on the day
errors.explanation: Prepositional calque from Spanish "depender de".
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: - "depender de" (Spanish) uses "de" which students map to "of"; English requires "on"

errors.original: the meeting is being very productive
errors.corrected: the meeting is very productive" or "meetings are very productive
errors.explanation: Overuse of progressive aspect with stative meaning. The context describes a general pattern, not an action in progress.
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: partial - Spanish "esta siendo" (progressive of ser/estar) is sometimes used similarly, but this is also a developmental error

errors.original: I think is a mix of both
errors.corrected: I think it is a mix of both
errors.explanation: Repeated null subject omission in embedded clause.
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: - pro-drop transfer

errors.original: On the other hand, the data analysts... On the other hand, the managers...
errors.corrected: On the other hand, the data analysts... At the same time / Meanwhile, the managers...
errors.explanation: Overuse of "on the other hand" for non-contrastive additions.
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: partial - Spanish "por otro lado" is used more broadly

errors.original: The meeting yesterday about this topic was being very long
errors.corrected: The meeting yesterday about this topic was very long
errors.explanation: Progressive aspect misuse with stative predicate "be long".
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: partial - "estaba siendo muy largo" is possible in Spanish

errors.original: the results were very sensible to how you measure them
errors.corrected: the results were very sensitive to how you measure them
errors.explanation: False friend: "sensible" (Spanish) = sensitive; "sensible" (English) = reasonable/practical.
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: - "sensible" (Spanish) = sensitive; "sensato" (Spanish) = sensible (English)

errors.original: I was discussing about this topic
errors.corrected: I was discussing this topic
errors.explanation: Redundant preposition after transitive verb "discuss".
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: - "discutir sobre un tema" (Spanish) requires the preposition; English "discuss" takes a direct object

errors.original: My boss is very comprehensive about my English mistakes
errors.corrected: My boss is very understanding about my English mistakes
errors.explanation: False friend: "comprensivo" (Spanish) = understanding; "comprehensive" (English) = thorough/complete.
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: - "comprensivo" (Spanish) = understanding/tolerant; "comprehensive" (English) = complete/all-encompassing

errors.original: I'm very interested on
errors.corrected: I'm very interested in
errors.explanation: Preposition error, self-corrected.
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: - "interesado en" (Spanish) maps to "interested in" but student initially chose "on"

errors.original: We need to make research on this
errors.corrected: We need to do research on this
errors.explanation: Collocation error: "do research" not "make research".
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: - "hacer investigacion" (Spanish) = make research; English uses "do" or "conduct"

errors.original: If I would work from home...
errors.corrected: If I could choose..." / "If I worked from home...
errors.explanation: Use of "would" in the if-clause of a second conditional. English requires past simple after "if".
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: - Spanish uses conditional in both clauses: "Si yo trabajaria" is colloquially heard (though also incorrect in standard Spanish)

errors.original: I think is terrible
errors.corrected: I think it is terrible
errors.explanation: Repeated null subject omission - third instance of this pattern.
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: - pro-drop

errors.original: I think is worth considering" / "the problem is that is very difficult
errors.corrected: I think it is worth considering" / "the problem is that it is very difficult
errors.explanation: Two null subject omissions in one turn - fourth and fifth instances.
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: - pro-drop

errors.original: it depend on many factors
errors.corrected: it depends on many factors
errors.explanation: Missing third-person singular -s.
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: partial - Spanish verb conjugation is richer, so the -s marker may seem redundant

errors.original: dependent of technology
errors.corrected: dependent on technology
errors.explanation: Same prepositional calque pattern as #6 - "de" mapped to "of".
errors.l1_transfer_explanation: - "dependiente de" (Spanish) -> "dependent of" instead of "dependent on"
